sources.excel: Fremantle 2025 Segmentation AI-Ready Format 2026_05 V2.xlsx — Persona Info sheet (102 columns, most recent data)
sources.pptx: Fremantle_Personas_050426_V1.2.pptx — Slides 13-20 (earlier data cut for brands, sole source for LTV/FPS/membership movements)

excel_fields.Profile: Breezy Boomers fans are confident and easygoing consumers who enjoy the rewards of a stable financial situation. They gravitate toward brands that offer convenience, reliability, and a sense of familiarity, favouring products or services that enhance their leisure-focused lifestyle. Tech-capable but not overly dependent on digital trends, they appreciate straightforward communication and value-for-money offerings that cater to their preferences for comfort and quality.
excel_fields.Consumer Attributes:
”Breezy Boomer" fans:
Relaxed and Confident: Approaches life with a laid-back attitude, valuing comfort and ease in their choices.
Financially Stable: Likely to have a steady income or retirement savings, allowing for discretionary spending on leisure and quality.
Experience-Oriented: Prefers enjoyable experiences over material possessions, such as travel, dining, or social activities.
Value-Conscious: Looks for good value without compromising on quality, favouring trusted and reliable brands.
Cautiously Tech-Capable: Comfortable using technology for convenience (e.g., online shopping or smart devices) but approaches new tech trends with caution, preferring familiar tools and platforms.
Health and Wellness Aware: Interested in products and services that promote well-being, comfort, and an active lifestyle.
Community-Focused: Enjoys being part of social groups or engaging in community events, whether locally or through online communities.
Appreciates Simplicity: Favours straightforward, no-frills communication and solutions that align with their relaxed, low-stress outlook.
excel_fields.Bio: Robert and Susan are Fremantle to the core and love being part of the Purple Army, with Robert now a 20+ year member.  They live a comfortable and indulgent lifestyle having downsized to a low maintenance townhouse close to cafes, restaurants, museums, galleries and the theatre. With ample time and financial resources from superannuation and a healthy share portfolio, they enjoy going to the football while also appreciating the finer things in life.  Now that their kids are young adults and have left the nest, they have the freedom to explore their passions and interests which include good food and wine and embracing the arts and they are regular visitors to Perth’s Cultural Centre.  Robert comes from a large Italian family and enjoys nothing more than celebrating family events over big Italian lunches at restaurants like La Sosta (one of his favourite places) and talking up Freo’s chances of winning a flag.
excel_fields.About Me:
Being able to support Fremantle as member is important to me.
When it comes to new technology, I am not an early adopter.
I’m proud of my heritage and culture.
When I buy, I’m looking for good quality at a fair price.
Being able to enjoy the finer things in life is important to me.

excel_fields.Cultural Diversity: Moderate: The Breezy Boomer have a moderate level of cultural diversity with a strong representation of second-generation Australians with a European heritage particularly Italy and Greece.  The Breezy Boomers have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population but slightly less than the Fremantle member base and index high on females.  Religious affiliations are less diverse with a bias towards Christianity.
excel_fields.Sustainability: Moderate: Breezy Boomers fans are aware of the importance of Sustainability and are reasonably proactive in supporting Sustainability initiatives. They invest in solar panels, conserve water, recycle diligently, and take particular care around household chemicals – reading labels carefully and disposing of hazardous products responsibly. Breezy Boomers can often be seen volunteering for community clean-up events near where they live.
excel_fields.Political Values: Conservative: Breezy Boomers fans hold strong and settled political views. Firmly conservative in outlook, they back parties that reflect their core beliefs – individual freedom, private enterprise, and a robust national defence are non-negotiable priorities for this segment.
excel_fields.Outlook on Life: Slightly Negative to Neutral: The Breezy Boomers have a neutral Outlook on Life, trending towards slightly negative.  They see themselves as generally a bit better off than most but are not overly positive about the future even though they are generally good in health. Breezy Boomers expect little to change from the way things are for themselves and the world.

pptx_only_fields.Membership Growth 2024-2025: Segment grew 16% in net numbers from 2024 — combination of movements from other segments plus solid growth in new/returning members
pptx_only_fields.Churn Rate 2024-2025 (actual): 10% actual churn — much lower than club baseline of 22%, lowest churn of all segments
pptx_only_fields.FPS Descriptors: High spend profile; Strong attendance profile; Good tenure profile; Responsive to marketing
pptx_only_fields.Health Status: Good; Term health issue; Follows healthy diet; Exercises regularly
pptx_only_fields.Lifestyle Quick Tags: Kids are mostly grown up; Well insured; Buys wine; Health food consumer; Exercises regularly; Recycles and buys earth friendly products
pptx_only_fields.Spend Quick Tags: Medical insurance; Car / vehicle; Rates / Body Corporate; Donations to charities; Books; Groceries / Household items
pptx_only_fields.Media Engagement Narrative: Breezy Boomers media exposure is primarily traditional with low participation in digital media. Reasonably engaged across club channels: 71% opted in for email, but open rates (52%) and click rates (15%) are quite low.
pptx_only_fields.Gender Note: Most likely male; Higher % males vs Australian population but slightly less than Fremantle member base; females at club average
pptx_only_fields.Population Estimate: 20-21% of Australian population; 21-22% of WA population
pptx_only_fields.Data Currency Note: PPTX brand indices are from an earlier data cut than Excel. Excel (Breezy Boomers sheet) is more recent. Where values differ, Excel takes precedence.

brand_spend_propensity.source: PPTX Slide 18 (310 brands — complete visual spend propensity table)
brand_spend_propensity.note: Excel Breezy Boomers sheet had external formula links that returned blanks. PPTX values used for full table. A few values differ slightly from Excel where Excel had more recent data (e.g. Dome Cafe: Excel=5.9x, PPTX=5.0x).
